summary: Closed, unmerged PR that claimed 100% objdiff matches for nine fighter functions: three Crazy Hand functions in ftCh_Init, ftCo_Rebirth_Cam in ft_0D31, and five Popo/Ice Climbers special-move functions in ftPp_SpecialS. The diff also bundled a large Claude Code workflow document and autonomous decompilation scripts, making it a mixed decomp/tooling/docs PR. There were no review comments or reviews in the slice, and no recorded reason for the rapid close.
key_files: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c - replaced stubs for ftCh_Slam_Anim, ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim, and ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670; added needed extern float constants and ftCo_Attack100 include
src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c - implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Cam camera subject update
src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c - implemented five Popo special-move functions
src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/types.h - split padding to add ftIceClimberAttributes.x80 and ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw fields
src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.h - declared fn_80122D2C with address comment
CLAUDE.md - added repository/decomp workflow guidance for Claude Code
scripts/decomp_helpers.py - helper subcommands for overnight automation
scripts/find_stubs.py and scripts/find_all_stubs.sh - stub discovery tooling
scripts/schedule_overnight.sh - macOS launchd scheduler for overnight runs
what_changed: Replaced Crazy Hand stubs: ftCh_Slam_Anim checks boss-lib/frame-ending conditions, clears self velocity to ftCh_Init_804DA178, sets a fighter flag, zeroes fp->x1A5C, and transitions through ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015A888; ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim handles boss cancel and frame-end motion transition to state 0x180; ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670 changes to state 0x151, sets invisible/accessory callback state, calls ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 0x1FF), sets fp->x2220_b3, and restarts animation.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Cam using a stack UnkFloat6_Camera, fp->x890_cameraBox, ftCamera_80076018, camera_box->x10 position fields, and ftLib_800866DC.

Implemented Popo/Ice Climbers ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_IASA and ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_IASA with matching bodies: clear cmd_vars[0], compare ABS(lstick.x) against new dat_attrs->x80, update facing, and rotate part Y by M_PI_2 * facing_dir.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter: reset throw_flags, cmd_vars[0], cmd_vars[3], mv.pp.speciallw fields, enter ftPp_MS_SpecialLw, call ftAnim_8006EBA4, and assign accessory4_cb = fn_80122D2C.

Implemented ftPp_SpecialLw_Anim and ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Anim: on animation end, destroy effects if fv.pp.x2230_b0 is set, clear callbacks/rotation, then transition to ft_8008A2BC on ground or ftCo_Fall_Enter in air.
Updated Popo types/header to expose the new attribute threshold x80, speciallw motion-vars layout, and fn_80122D2C declaration needed by the C implementations.

Added Claude-oriented decomp workflow material and scripts for finding stubs, batching small targets, monitoring Claude stream JSON, parsing results/token usage/rate limits, using worktrees, and optionally creating PRs.
smart_moves: The C changes mostly reuse existing fighter helpers and fields instead of raw control-flow reconstruction: ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftCommon_UpdateFacing, ftPartSetRotY, efLib_DestroyAll, ftCo_Fall_Enter, and ft_8008A2BC.

The Popo attribute threshold was promoted from padding to ftIceClimberAttributes.x80, and special-lw motion variables were added to ftPp_MotionVars, which is more reusable than indexing into anonymous pads.

The ground/air Popo IASA implementations share the same structure, and the ground/air SpecialLw anim implementations differ only in the final transition, matching the common decomp pattern for paired action states.
PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(16) were used in several Popo functions, indicating stack-frame matching attention.
The added automation separated reusable Python helper subcommands from the shell runner and used structured stream JSON parsing instead of only grepping conversational output.
The automation’s stub finder sorted targets by symbol size and could batch small functions from the same file, which is a practical tactic for low-risk decomp progress.
decomp_lessons: Keep decomp PRs focused. This slice mixed nine fighter decompilations with 1000+ lines of Claude docs/automation; because the PR closed unmerged and had no review, future work should avoid bundling unrelated tooling with match patches unless explicitly requested.

When a function naturally needs a field inside padding, split the pad and add the smallest named struct field rather than using anonymous byte access; this PR did that for ftIceClimberAttributes.x80 and ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw.
For paired ground/air fighter states, compare nearby variants and preserve identical bodies where possible, changing only the terminal ground/air transition when the assembly calls different helpers.
Stack padding can be a deliberate matching tool, but future patches should justify PAD_STACK by frame-size comparison when review context is available.
The ftCo_Rebirth_Cam implementation contains a raw cast at fp->mv.co.common.x8; future cleanup should verify whether that motion-var field can be named/typed instead of retaining a cast.
Self-reported `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and 100% objdiff are useful, but unmerged PRs without reviewer confirmation should be treated as candidate evidence, not accepted project history.
naming_conventions: Function prefixes follow subsystem/character conventions: ftCh_ for Crazy Hand, ftPp_ for Popo/Ice Climbers, and ftCo_ for common fighter code.
Unknown helper functions may remain address-based with fn_80122D2C plus an address comment when no semantic name is established.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style names such as x80, x0, x4_b0, and padding names such as _7C; bitfields use suffixes like _b0.

The added CLAUDE.md documents broader conventions: file names lowerCamelCase, functions UpperCamelCase after module prefix, and common abbreviations such as cb, coll, dmg, lr, mv, pos, sfx, spd, and vel.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_IASA, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_IASA, ftPp_SpecialLw_Anim, and ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Anim; used PAD_STACK(8) in ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter.
Used ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) > da->x80 to express an input magnitude threshold cleanly.
Used chained assignment fp->self_vel.x = fp->self_vel.y = fp->self_vel.z = ftCh_Init_804DA178 for the Crazy Hand slam velocity clear.
Re-fetched fp after efLib_DestroyAll before clearing fv.pp.x2230_b0 and callbacks, preserving the observed operation order in the implemented Popo anim functions.

CLAUDE.md added general tactics: prefer true matches over fake pointer arithmetic, convert simple do/while loops to for loops, avoid gotos, split padding for real fields, ignore pure register swaps for later permuter work, use PAD_STACK for stack-size differences, and use ABS/MIN/MAX macros for common patterns.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
The PR was CLOSED with merged_at null about four minutes after creation; the dump gives no explicit closure or rejection reason.
The only verification evidence is the PR body’s self-reported checklist: `ninja` builds, `ninja diff` has no regressions, and all functions are 100% matches in objdiff.
follow_up_queries: Was PR #2276 replaced by later smaller PRs that landed any of these nine fighter functions?
Search current master/history for ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim, ftCh_Slam_Anim, ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670, ftCo_Rebirth_Cam, and the five ftPp_Special* functions before reusing this diff.
Verify whether ftIceClimberAttributes.x80 and ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw names/layout survived in later accepted code.
Check whether CLAUDE.md or the overnight decomp scripts were intentionally rejected or simply closed without discussion.
Review ftCo_Rebirth_Cam’s fp->mv.co.common.x8 cast and determine whether the motion-var struct should expose a typed f32 field.
